title: Activation Test
wikitext:
[]: Activation Test             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:23 pm Do we know if an initial activation test can be performed using a dummy plug? I'm writing an article for it and I'd like some input. Also, if a human is determined to be necessary, that means a heck of a lot for Eva-04. Personally, I think that all evidence points to a necessary pilot, because every initial test that we see in the series has involved one. I've seen speculation that Eva-04's test could have been dummy plugged, and I'm not sure to agree or disagree with it. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:32 pm MP Evas? Or it doesn't apply in this case? I don't think it has been fully explained but i think the dummy plug can do the work fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:34 pm Sounds good, assuming that the first time the MPEs were activated was in the sky. (I would believe this.) The question, then: Was there a plug in Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:53 pm Probably there was a plug but again we will never know. Hard to believe the unit didn't have a plug though. I always thought they come pre-installed. The thing is we don't know if Unit 3 or Unit 4 had a plug during the transportation or got one when they arrived. Assuming there wasn't a plug can we speculate that this was the reason the S2 engine failed upon activation and caused the destruction? Assuming that the failure of S2 engine was because of an accident and not something else.... Or maybe they used a dummy one and it caused the destruction. In general in NGE everything about Unit 4 is a mystery...

Article: Activation Test
[]: Activation Test             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:23 pm Do we know if an initial activation test can be performed using a dummy plug? I'm writing an article for it and I'd like some input. Also, if a human is determined to be necessary, that means a heck of a lot for Eva-04. Personally, I think that all evidence points to a necessary pilot, because every initial test that we see in the series has involved one. I've seen speculation that Eva-04's test could have been dummy plugged, and I'm not sure to agree or disagree with it. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:32 pm MP Evas? Or it doesn't apply in this case? I don't think it has been fully explained but i think the dummy plug can do the work fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:34 pm Sounds good, assuming that the first time the MPEs were activated was in the sky. (I would believe this.) The question, then: Was there a plug in Eva-04?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Mar 18, 2014 5:53 pm Probably there was a plug but again we will never know. Hard to believe the unit didn't have a plug though. I always thought they come pre-installed. The thing is we don't know if Unit 3 or Unit 4 had a plug during the transportation or got one when they arrived. Assuming there wasn't a plug can we speculate that this was the reason the S2 engine failed upon activation and caused the destruction? Assuming that the failure of S2 engine was because of an accident and not something else.... Or maybe they used a dummy one and it caused the destruction. In general in NGE everything about Unit 4 is a mystery...
